ocr.text: The following table presents a comprehensive analysis of the relationship between various karyotypes and comorbid conditions
as assessed by the Charlson Comorbidity Index.
Variables  Adjusted HRa  (95% CI)  P value
Charlson Comorbidity Index  1.77  (1.57–2.00)  <0.001
Karyotype  FISH [no. of cells]  Group A (no. of cases)  Group B (no.of cases)
46,XX  nuc ish (DXZ1x1)//(DXZ1x2)*  2  4
46,XY  nuc ish (DXZ1x1)[77]//(DXZ1,DYZ3)x1[123]  0  1
mos 45,X/46,XY  nuc ish (DXZ1x1)[299]//(DXZ1,DYZ3)x1[65]//(DXZ1x1,DYZ3x2)[36]  0  1
Sample #  % Soluble Proteins/FS  % Collagen/Proteins  % GAG/FS  % Collagen/FS  RC/GAG
This index provides a robust metric for evaluating the impact of comorbidities on patient outcomes, specifically indicating a
hazard ratio (HR) of 1.77, with a 95% confidence interval (CI) ranging from 1.57 to 2.00, and a statistically significant p-value
of less than 0.001. The data also elucidate the distribution of cases across two distinct groups—Group A and Group B—
highlighting the prevalence of specific karyotypes, such as 46,XX and those identified through fluorescence in situ
hybridization (FISH) methods. Important metrics, including the percentage of soluble proteins relative to the total fraction of
soluble proteins (FS) and collagen-to-protein ratios, are included to provide further insights into the biochemical landscape
associated with these conditions. Collectively, these variables underscore the importance of integrating genetic and biochemical
markers in understanding patient profiles and their associated risks.
